mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks including text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a decentralized and local-first AI solution.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the LocalAI deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai-linux-amd64

sudo mv localai-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/localai

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step sets up a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI using a Helm chart, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the LocalAI deployment and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Backup your data, upgrade the LocalAI deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl cp localai/localai-pod:/data /backup/localai-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup existing data before upgrading.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there is a network issue.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your network is working.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Minikube cluster is running and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Minikube cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory limits when starting Minikube.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
